summary: Decent audience recording captures a lively 1974 East‑Coast show that many fans remember as an awesome performance.
review: The tape is an audience source with decent clarity – not a soundboard but good enough to convey the concert atmosphere.  Fans describe the night as a fantastic, high‑energy Dead show (two 5‑star rave reviews) though one reviewer calls it only average by the era’s standards.  Overall the performance shines thanks to the band’s tight playing and the memorable vibe of the summer ’74 tour.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong fan enthusiasm and several 5‑star reviews indicate a solid show; recording is an audience tape that is decent but not pristine, yielding a combined rating around four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audience tape with decent clarity and some ambient crowd noise; not a soundboard recording but captures the live atmosphere well enough for enjoyment.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical 1974 East‑Coast tour energy – concise numbers mixed with longer jams, delivering an overall enjoyable and lively set despite one reviewer finding it merely average for the period.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mentioned dancing onstage next to him; no specific critique of his playing.
